LLM Logs

Every call to the Claude API during invoice processing is recorded in an LLM log entry. These logs let you audit AI extraction activity, monitor token usage and costs, understand why a particular invoice was extracted the way it was, and track which model tier handled each invoice.

What is logged

One row is written to the llm_logs table for each extraction attempt. A single invoice may have two rows if the fast model's result was unsatisfactory and the strong model was tried as a fallback. Each row records:

FieldNotes
ModelThe Claude model ID that handled this call (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5-20251001)
Prompt tokensNumber of tokens in the input sent to Claude
Completion tokensNumber of tokens in Claude's response
Duration (ms)Time from request sent to response received, in milliseconds
ConfidenceThe confidence score Claude returned for this extraction (0–1)
Linked documentThe invoice this log entry belongs to (polymorphic link)
ErrorError message if the call failed, otherwise empty

The full request and response payloads (including the prompt sent to Claude and Claude's raw JSON response) are also stored but are only shown in detail views, not in the list.

The log list

Go to Settings › LLM Logs (/llm-logs) to see a read-only table of all extraction attempts. The table shows the model used, confidence score, token counts, duration, linked invoice, and whether the call succeeded or failed. You can search and filter the list. The LLM Logs page is read-only — entries cannot be edited or deleted.

Reading a log entry

When reviewing a log entry, the most important fields are:

  • Model — which model tier handled the call. A Haiku model ID means the fast model succeeded. A Sonnet model ID means the fast model was either unavailable or produced an unsatisfactory result and the strong model was used.
  • Confidence — Claude's self-reported confidence in its extraction. Values close to 1.0 indicate high confidence; values below the fallback_confidence threshold (default 0.80) will have triggered a fallback to the strong model.
  • Token counts — useful for estimating API cost per invoice. Prompt tokens reflect the length of the invoice text; completion tokens reflect the size of the extracted data.
  • Error — if present, this tells you why the call failed. A 404 not_found_error means the model ID has been retired; see Configuration — Model health and fallback.

For aggregated metrics across many invoices, use the LLM Performance report.

Permissions

  • llm-logs-view-any — to access the LLM Logs list
  • llm-logs-view — to view a single log entry in detail
  • view-llm-summary — to see the AI confidence summary displayed on individual invoices and in reports
